summary: Explosive Lost Sailor>Saint jam, blazing Shakedown Street, tight set
blurb: The second set erupts with a scorching Lost Sailor > Saint > Spanish Jam and a blistering Shakedown Street that drive the night forward. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Brent supplies sizzling keys, making this Cornell show one of the era’s most energetic performances.
review: May 16 1981 at Barton Hall remains a high‑water mark for the early ’80s Dead. The first set rolls smoothly through classics like Friend Of The Devil and Big River before building momentum into a soaring Let It Grow that segues into an unforgettable second half. Once Shakedown Street kicks in, the band launches into a marathon of improvisation – the Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance > Spanish Jam sequence is nothing short of explosive, with Jerry’s lead tones “on his game” and Brent trading scorching licks that keep the groove alive. Highlights such as High Time, Stella Blue and an encore Uncle John’s Band showcase a band locked in, while the rhythm section—Mickey’s playful chants during Drums and Billy’s solid back‑beat—adds extra fire. Even with a few minor slip‑ups on Friend Of The Devil and Althea, the overall energy and tightness make this concert a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across the majority of recordings (7 out of 9) praising the Lost Sailor > Saint jam, Shakedown Street, Jerry’s fiery guitar and Brent’s sizzling keys. Only one mixed and one negative source, both focusing on recording flaws rather than performance, so overall show sentiment is highly favorable.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard capture with crisp, well‑balanced mix that highlights the explosive Lost Sailor > Saint > Spanish Jam and tight playing throughout.

key_highlights: Explosive Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance > Spanish Jam sequence
Blazing Shakedown Street with call‑and‑response between Jerry and Brent
Soaring Let It Grow and High Time that lift the second set
Tight rhythm section – Mickey’s chanting Drums, Billy’s solid backbeat

band_performance.Jerry: On his game the entire night – fiery, razor‑sharp leads on Shakedown, Lost Sailor and a majestic tone on Let It Grow; a brief dip in energy after the break is noted but overall performance stays locked in.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, engaged bass foundation throughout, especially evident on Passenger and during the extended jam sections.
band_performance.Bob: Locks into the groove on Truckin', adds haunting intro notes to Shakedown, and contributes a Hendrix‑style impression on solo moments.
band_performance.Brent: Sizzling keyboards and vocal harmonies; his solos on Big River and interplay with Jerry during the Spanish Jam are consistently praised as ‘smokin’ and ‘just smokin'’.
band_performance.Mickey: Playful chanting during Drums and a crisp drum break into Space add character to the improvisational flow.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers steady, driving drumming that underpins the high‑energy second set and keeps the band’s momentum tight.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
